name: Sicilian Tomato Pesto with Linguine (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 2 large ripe red tomatoes, peeled and seeded, chopped and drained
coarse salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the pine nuts and cook, stirring constantly, until they turn golden, about 3 to 4 minutes. Remove them from the pan immediately.
2. Place the basil leaves, garlic, pine nuts, and olive oil in a blender or food processor. Blend at high speed until smooth. Scrape down the sides of the container if necessary.
3. Add half of the Parmigiano-Reggiano and red pepper flakes to the blender. Pulse a few times to make a thick paste.
4. Fold in the chopped tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and blend again briefly.
5. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the linguine until al dente, about 7 to 9 minutes. Drain, reserving some pasta cooking liquid.
6. Toss the cooked linguine with the pesto sauce, adding more reserved pasta water if needed for desired consistency.
7. Serve immediately, sprinkled with the remaining Parmigiano-Reggiano.
